# Phase 5: Work Items Inbox Verification Report

**Phase Goal:** Users have a functional inbox where they can view, complete, and reject tasks generated by the workflow engine, with basic performer assignment routing tasks to the right users
**Verified:** 2026-03-31
**Status:** passed
**Re-verification:** No — initial verification

## Goal Achievement

| 2  | WorkItemComment model with work_item_id, user_id, content fields | VERIFIED | `workflow.py:159-170` — class with FK columns and Text content |
| 3  | WorkItem has comments relationship back_populates to WorkItemComment | VERIFIED | `workflow.py:156: comments: Mapped[list["WorkItemComment"]] = relationship(back_populates="work_item")` |
| 4  | resolve_performers maps SUPERVISOR/USER/GROUP to user IDs | VERIFIED | `engine_service.py:135-169` — full match/case implementation with real DB query for GROUP |
| 5  | Engine creates one work item per resolved performer | VERIFIED | `engine_service.py:454: for perf_id in performers:` — fan-out loop in _advance_from_activity |
| 6  | Inbox Pydantic schemas include nested activity, workflow, document summaries | VERIFIED | `schemas/inbox.py` — 10 schema classes including ActivitySummary, WorkflowSummary, DocumentSummary, InboxItemResponse, InboxItemDetailResponse |
| 7  | GET /api/v1/inbox returns user's pending work items with nested context | VERIFIED | `inbox_service.py:34` — real DB query with selectinload chains; test_work_item_appears_in_inbox passes |
| 8  | Work item lifecycle (acquire/release/complete/reject) enforced correctly | VERIFIED | inbox_service enforces ACQUIRED state before complete/reject; SELECT FOR UPDATE on acquire; all 11 tests pass |
| 9  | Comments can be added and listed | VERIFIED | `inbox_service.py:399,443` — add_comment and get_comments with real DB operations |
| 10 | Inbox router with 8 endpoints registered at /api/v1/inbox | VERIFIED | `main.py:84: application.include_router(inbox.router, prefix=settings.api_v1_prefix)` — 8 service calls in router |

**Score:** 10/10 truths verified

| Artifact | Data Variable | Source | Produces Real Data | Status |

| `inbox_service.get_inbox_items` | `work_items` list | `select(WorkItem).join(...).where(performer_id == user_id)` | Yes — real DB query with joins through ActivityInstance, WorkflowInstance, ProcessTemplate | FLOWING |
| `inbox_service.get_inbox_item_detail` | `wi` (single WorkItem) | `select(WorkItem)` with selectinload chains | Yes — DB query with 4 selectinload levels | FLOWING |
| `inbox_service.acquire_work_item` | `wi` state transition | `select(WorkItem).with_for_update()` then state mutation | Yes — SELECT FOR UPDATE, state written to DB | FLOWING |
| `inbox_service.complete_inbox_item` | workflow advancement | delegates to `engine_service.complete_work_item` | Yes — engine queries workflow, advances state | FLOWING |
| `resolve_performers GROUP` | `performers` list | `select(user_groups.c.user_id).where(group_id == ...)` | Yes — real user_groups table query | FLOWING |

| INBOX-05 | 05-02, 05-03 | User can reject a work item | SATISFIED | reject_inbox_item sets state=REJECTED, completed_at, creates audit record; test_reject_work_item passes |

| INBOX-07 | 05-01, 05-03 | Work items show priority and due date indicators | SATISFIED | InboxItemResponse includes priority (int) and due_date (datetime or None) fields; test_inbox_shows_priority_due_date passes |
| PERF-01 | 05-01, 05-02, 05-03 | Activity can be assigned to Workflow Supervisor | SATISFIED | resolve_performers case "supervisor" returns workflow.supervisor_id; test_supervisor_performer passes |

| PERF-03 | 05-01, 05-02, 05-03 | Activity can be assigned to users from a Group (parallel execution) | SATISFIED | resolve_performers case "group" queries user_groups table, engine creates one work item per member; test_group_performer verifies both admin and regular user see work items |

All 10 requirements: SATISFIED. No orphaned requirements for Phase 5.

### Anti-Patterns Found

None found. Scanned all 5 phase-modified files for TODO, FIXME, placeholder comments, empty returns, and hardcoded empty data flowing to user-visible output. All clear.

### Human Verification Required

The following items cannot be fully verified programmatically and should be spot-checked by a human before marking the phase production-ready:

#### 1. Reject Flow Scope Boundary

**Test:** Reject a work item via POST /api/v1/inbox/{id}/reject and confirm the workflow does NOT advance (no reject routing, no next activity triggered).
**Expected:** Workflow remains at the same activity; only the rejected work item transitions to REJECTED state; no new work items created.
**Why human:** The plan explicitly notes "does NOT trigger reject flow routing (Phase 6)" — confirming this non-behavior requires tracing workflow state after rejection, which is technically testable but the current test (test_reject_work_item) only checks the work item state, not the downstream workflow state.

#### 2. Concurrent Acquire Race Condition

**Test:** Simulate two users simultaneously acquiring the same AVAILABLE work item.
**Expected:** One succeeds (state becomes ACQUIRED), the other gets a 400 error.
**Why human:** The SELECT FOR UPDATE is implemented but correctness under actual concurrent load requires two simultaneous requests, which cannot be tested with single-threaded async_client.

### Gaps Summary

No gaps. All must-haves verified at all levels. Phase goal is fully achieved: users have a functional inbox where they can view, complete, and reject tasks generated by the workflow engine, with performer assignment routing tasks to user, supervisor, and group performer types. 161 tests pass with zero regressions.
